Coleoptera Karyotype Database

4,960 records · 252 publications · All karyotype databases

Loading database…

–
records match filters

A karyotype is a highly variable and complex trait that can reveal changes in genome organization, uncover phylogenetic history, and distinguish cryptic species. This database contains 4,960 Coleoptera karyotypes compiled from 252 publications. Use the filters on the left to narrow records by suborder, family, genus, or sex chromosome system. All tabs - summary stats, table, and plot - update instantly.

Submitting data: If you are aware of records that should be added, please contact us.

Data taken from this database must not be reproduced in published lists, online databases, or other formats without permission. Provided solely for personal and academic use.

Citation: Blackmon, H. and Demuth, J.P. (2015). Coleoptera Karyotype Database. The Coleopterists Bulletin, 69(1), 174–175.

Version 1.4 · Last updated 30 October 2020
